phrases: how do i get the next chapter
where do i get the next chapter

top_24h.caption:
Heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment 
“NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll DM you a 3-minute video to see if you qualify for one of the 10 free Clarity Calls I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover 
the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

The #1 most common pattern I see keeping men stuck after heartbreak is breaking the first commandment… 
Let me explain.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:
“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, 
and all these things will be added to you.” 
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes. 

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

Heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment 
“NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll DM you a 3-minute video to see if you qualify for one of the 10 free Clarity Calls I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover 
the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment 
“NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll DM you a 3-minute video to see if you qualify for one of the 10 free Clarity Calls I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover 
the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

Heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

top_30d.caption:
You think the problem is that you care too much.
It is not.
A lot of men who look strong in public become unstable in private for one reason: they let a woman sit in a place she was never meant to hold.
You can see it in the small things.
Checking your phone to see if she responded.
 Feeling your whole internal state change because she sounded distant.
 Reading her mood to decide whether you get to feel steady today.
 Needing her approval to feel secure again.
Most men call that love.
It is not love.
It is dependency dressed up in romantic language.
And the reason it feels so intense is because you are not just asking her for connection. You are asking her for peace. For reassurance. For identity. For emotional stability.
That is why it gets so heavy.
Because no woman was built to carry that much weight for a man.
And she can feel it.
She can feel when your peace is sitting in her hands.
 She can feel when your nervous system is asking her to stabilize you.
 She can feel when being loved by you starts to feel more like managing you.
That does not make her feel cherished.
It makes her feel responsible.
Here is the part most men never name:
You are asking her to do God’s job.
That is why the relationship starts to suffocate.
The issue is not that you love too deeply.
 The issue is that your deepest love is pointed at the wrong source.
Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.
So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife,
I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.
Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step.

Read that again.

And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes. 

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

You might want to punch me in the face for being this brutally honest…

But if you’re hurting right now, I need you to read this all the way through.

Because most people aren’t suffering for the reason they think they are.

Not after the breakup.
Not after the job loss.
Not after the dream collapsed.

The pain hits like a truck, and you don’t know why it won’t leave.
You pray, journal, cry, but it still lingers.

Let me show you something that flipped everything for me:

James 4:13–14 says,

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist…”

Most people think the pain is from the event.
But what if it’s not?

What if…
It’s from the future you built in your mind,
The one that never happened.
The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The version of life you were so sure God had promised.

That future felt real. But it was only an illusion.

And when it vanished, you didn’t just lose what was…
You lost what you thought would be.

That’s why the Bible urges us not to say, “I’ll do this, I’ll do that.”
But instead, “If it is the Lord’s will…”

Surrender doesn’t mean giving up.

It means breathing again.

It means waking up here instead of chasing what should’ve been.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.
And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover the limiting belief behind your heartbreak 
and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

“The more you love her, the better the relationship will be” is the lie that causes Christian divorce. That’s not God’s design. On the contrary, it’s actually the biggest danger to a christian marriage.

Not dangerous in an obvious way. Dangerous because they give everything. They prioritize her above everything. They make her their peace, their security, their purpose. 

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step.

And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step.

Heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

You might want to punch me in the face for being this brutally honest…

But if you’re hurting right now, I need you to read this all the way through.

Because most people aren’t suffering for the reason they think they are.

Not after the breakup.
Not after the job loss.
Not after the dream collapsed.

The pain hits like a truck, and you don’t know why it won’t leave.
You pray, journal, cry, but it still lingers.

Let me show you something that flipped everything for me:

James 4:13–14 says,

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist…”

Most people think the pain is from the event.
But what if it’s not?

What if…
It’s from the future you built in your mind,
The one that never happened.
The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The version of life you were so sure God had promised.

That future felt real. But it was only an illusion.

And when it vanished, you didn’t just lose what was…
You lost what you thought would be.

That’s why the Bible urges us not to say, “I’ll do this, I’ll do that.”
But instead, “If it is the Lord’s will…”

Surrender doesn’t mean giving up.

It means breathing again.

It means waking up here instead of chasing what should’ve been.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.
And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

You think the problem is that you care too much.
It is not.
A lot of men who look strong in public become unstable in private for one reason: they let a woman sit in a place she was never meant to hold.
You can see it in the small things.
Checking your phone to see if she responded.
 Feeling your whole internal state change because she sounded distant.
 Reading her mood to decide whether you get to feel steady today.
 Needing her approval to feel secure again.
Most men call that love.
It is not love.
It is dependency dressed up in romantic language.
And the reason it feels so intense is because you are not just asking her for connection. You are asking her for peace. For reassurance. For identity. For emotional stability.
That is why it gets so heavy.
Because no woman was built to carry that much weight for a man.
And she can feel it.
She can feel when your peace is sitting in her hands.
 She can feel when your nervous system is asking her to stabilize you.
 She can feel when being loved by you starts to feel more like managing you.
That does not make her feel cherished.
It makes her feel responsible.
Here is the part most men never name:
You are asking her to do God’s job.
That is why the relationship starts to suffocate.
The issue is not that you love too deeply.
 The issue is that your deepest love is pointed at the wrong source.
Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.
So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife,
I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

and I’ll DM you the first step.

You think the problem is that you care too much.
It is not.
A lot of men who look strong in public become unstable in private for one reason: they let a woman sit in a place she was never meant to hold.
You can see it in the small things.
Checking your phone to see if she responded.
 Feeling your whole internal state change because she sounded distant.
 Reading her mood to decide whether you get to feel steady today.
 Needing her approval to feel secure again.
Most men call that love.
It is not love.
It is dependency dressed up in romantic language.
And the reason it feels so intense is because you are not just asking her for connection. You are asking her for peace. For reassurance. For identity. For emotional stability.
That is why it gets so heavy.
Because no woman was built to carry that much weight for a man.
And she can feel it.
She can feel when your peace is sitting in her hands.
 She can feel when your nervous system is asking her to stabilize you.
 She can feel when being loved by you starts to feel more like managing you.
That does not make her feel cherished.
It makes her feel responsible.
Here is the part most men never name:
You are asking her to do God’s job.
That is why the relationship starts to suffocate.
The issue is not that you love too deeply.

“The more you love her, the better the relationship will be” is the lie that causes Christian divorce. That’s not God’s design. On the contrary, it’s actually the biggest danger to a christian marriage.

Not dangerous in an obvious way. Dangerous because they give everything. They prioritize her above everything. They make her their peace, their security, their purpose. 

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

Heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment

the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment 
“NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll DM you a 3-minute video to see if you qualify for one of the 10 free Clarity Calls I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover 
the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

God will keep breaking your heart until you stop believing this one lie.
Most heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

You might want to punch me in the face for being this brutally honest…

But if you’re hurting right now, I need you to read this all the way through.

Because most people aren’t suffering for the reason they think they are.

Not after the breakup.
Not after the job loss.
Not after the dream collapsed.

The pain hits like a truck, and you don’t know why it won’t leave.
You pray, journal, cry, but it still lingers.

Let me show you something that flipped everything for me:

James 4:13–14 says,

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist…”

Most people think the pain is from the event.
But what if it’s not?

What if…
It’s from the future you built in your mind,
The one that never happened.
The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The version of life you were so sure God had promised.

That future felt real. But it was only an illusion.

And when it vanished, you didn’t just lose what was…
You lost what you thought would be.

That’s why the Bible urges us not to say, “I’ll do this, I’ll do that.”
But instead, “If it is the Lord’s will…”

Surrender doesn’t mean giving up.

It means breathing again.

It means waking up here instead of chasing what should’ve been.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.
And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.

I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover the limiting belief behind your heartbreak 
and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

You might want to punch me in the face for being this brutally honest…

But if you’re hurting right now, I need you to read this all the way through.

Because most people aren’t suffering for the reason they think they are.

Not after the breakup.
Not after the job loss.
Not after the dream collapsed.

The pain hits like a truck, and you don’t know why it won’t leave.
You pray, journal, cry, but it still lingers.

Let me show you something that flipped everything for me:

James 4:13–14 says,

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist…”

Most people think the pain is from the event.
But what if it’s not?

What if…
It’s from the future you built in your mind,
The one that never happened.
The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The version of life you were so sure God had promised.

That future felt real. But it was only an illusion.

And when it vanished, you didn’t just lose what was…
You lost what you thought would be.

That’s why the Bible urges us not to say, “I’ll do this, I’ll do that.”
But instead, “If it is the Lord’s will…”

Surrender doesn’t mean giving up.

It means breathing again.

It means waking up here instead of chasing what should’ve been.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.
And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern is where most 
Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

I asked 250 men who lost the love of their life what went wrong.
Every single one said it came back to the same problem.

The moment uncertainty entered the relationship, they stopped leading and started 
trying to control what they could not control.
And the behavior they thought would keep her… was usually 
the very thing that drove her away.

A lot of sharp, disciplined, successful men become unstable the moment uncertainty enters a relationship.
Then they turn into detectives.
You know the pattern:

You scan tone.
Analyze response times.
Look for proof something changed.

You call it vigilance. Or boundaries. Or standards.
It starts small.
You feel uncertainty, so you monitor.
You investigate.
You get controlling.

But then she feels watched.
She feels pressure.
She starts pulling back.

Then the fights start.
Then the distance grows.
And the more distance you feel, the more control you try to get back.

That’s the loop.

And most men don’t realize they’re feeding the exact thing they’re terrified of.
Because the fear isn’t really just about what she might do.
It’s that her uncertainty starts creating uncertainty in you.

Because somewhere along the way, your identity, and your hope for a good future 
got placed in whether or not she chooses you.

So when she feels uncertain, your spirit feels unstable.
And once a man starts looking to control her to calm himself, 
the relationship is already in danger.

That’s not love.
That’s idolatry.

The solution is not better monitoring.
It’s rebuilding identity, placing hope, and getting your validation of goodness 
from Christ.

They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

And the behavior they thought would keep her… was usually 
the very thing that drove her away.

A lot of sharp, disciplined, successful men become unstable the moment uncertainty enters a relationship.
Then they turn into detectives.
You know the pattern:

You scan tone.
Analyze response times.
Look for proof something changed.

You call it vigilance. Or boundaries. Or standards.
It starts small.
You feel uncertainty, so you monitor.
You investigate.
You get controlling.

But then she feels watched.
She feels pressure.
She starts pulling back.

Then the fights start.
Then the distance grows.
And the more distance you feel, the more control you try to get back.

That’s the loop.

And most men don’t realize they’re feeding the exact thing they’re terrified of.
Because the fear isn’t really just about what she might do.
It’s that her uncertainty starts creating uncertainty in you.

Because somewhere along the way, your identity, and your hope for a good future 
got placed in whether or not she chooses you.

So when she feels uncertain, your spirit feels unstable.
And once a man starts looking to control her to calm himself, 
the relationship is already in danger.

That’s not love.
That’s idolatry.

The solution is not better monitoring.
It’s rebuilding identity, placing hope, and getting your validation of goodness 
from Christ.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern is 
where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

God will keep breaking your heart until you stop believing this one lie.
Most men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because of the future they built in their imagination.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

You think the problem is that you care too much.
It is not.
A lot of men who look strong in public become unstable in private for one reason: they let a woman sit in a place she was never meant to hold.
You can see it in the small things.
Checking your phone to see if she responded.
 Feeling your whole internal state change because she sounded distant.
 Reading her mood to decide whether you get to feel steady today.
 Needing her approval to feel secure again.
Most men call that love.
It is not love.
It is dependency dressed up in romantic language.
And the reason it feels so intense is because you are not just asking her for connection. You are asking her for peace. For reassurance. For identity. For emotional stability.
That is why it gets so heavy.
Because no woman was built to carry that much weight for a man.
And she can feel it.
She can feel when your peace is sitting in her hands.
 She can feel when your nervous system is asking her to stabilize you.
 She can feel when being loved by you starts to feel more like managing you.
That does not make her feel cherished.
It makes her feel responsible.
Here is the part most men never name:
You are asking her to do God’s job.
That is why the relationship starts to suffocate.
The issue is not that you love too deeply.
 The issue is that your deepest love is pointed at the wrong source.
Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.
So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife,
I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.
Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step.

Checking your phone to see if she responded.
 Feeling your whole internal state change because she sounded distant.
 Reading her mood to decide whether you get to feel steady today.
 Needing her approval to feel secure again.
Most men call that love.
It is not love.
It is dependency dressed up in romantic language.
And the reason it feels so intense is because you are not just asking her for connection. You are asking her for peace. For reassurance. For identity. For emotional stability.
That is why it gets so heavy.
Because no woman was built to carry that much weight for a man.
And she can feel it.
She can feel when your peace is sitting in her hands.
 She can feel when your nervous system is asking her to stabilize you.
 She can feel when being loved by you starts to feel more like managing you.
That does not make her feel cherished.
It makes her feel responsible.
Here is the part most men never name:
You are asking her to do God’s job.
That is why the relationship starts to suffocate.
The issue is not that you love too deeply.
 The issue is that your deepest love is pointed at the wrong source.
Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.
So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife,
I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

“The more you love her, the better the relationship will be” is the lie that causes Christian divorce. That’s not God’s design. On the contrary, it’s actually the biggest danger to a christian marriage.

Not dangerous in an obvious way. Dangerous because they give everything. They prioritize her above everything. They make her their peace, their security, their purpose. 

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

Most men are trying to escape the very thing God is using to build them.

They treat pain like a problem to be solved.

They want it gone. They pray it away. They distract it away. And when it is still there on Tuesday morning, they conclude that something is broken, either in them or in God’s plan.

Here is what no one told you. The pain is not the problem. The pain is the equipment.

Think about a gym. You do not walk in hoping the weights feel lighter this week. You show up because resistance is the only thing that builds anything. Pull the weight. Feel the burn. Walk out stronger. That is not a punishment. That is the mechanism.

That is how strength works. And you think God operates differently?

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

Think about a gym. You do not walk in hoping the weights feel lighter this week. You show up because resistance is the only thing that builds anything. Pull the weight. Feel the burn. Walk out stronger. That is not a punishment. That is the mechanism.

That is how strength works. And you think God operates differently?

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful and hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

top_7d.caption:
God will keep breaking your heart until you stop believing this one lie.
Most heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment 
“NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll DM you a 3-minute video to see if you qualify for one of the 10 free Clarity Calls I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover 
the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

You might want to punch me in the face for being this brutally honest…

But if you’re hurting right now, I need you to read this all the way through.

Because most people aren’t suffering for the reason they think they are.

Not after the breakup.
Not after the job loss.
Not after the dream collapsed.

The pain hits like a truck, and you don’t know why it won’t leave.
You pray, journal, cry, but it still lingers.

Let me show you something that flipped everything for me:

James 4:13–14 says,

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist…”

Most people think the pain is from the event.
But what if it’s not?

What if…
It’s from the future you built in your mind,
The one that never happened.
The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The version of life you were so sure God had promised.

That future felt real. But it was only an illusion.

And when it vanished, you didn’t just lose what was…
You lost what you thought would be.

That’s why the Bible urges us not to say, “I’ll do this, I’ll do that.”
But instead, “If it is the Lord’s will…”

Surrender doesn’t mean giving up.

It means breathing again.

It means waking up here instead of chasing what should’ve been.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.
And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

You might want to punch me in the face for being this brutally honest…

But if you’re hurting right now, I need you to read this all the way through.

Because most people aren’t suffering for the reason they think they are.

Not after the breakup.
Not after the job loss.
Not after the dream collapsed.

The pain hits like a truck, and you don’t know why it won’t leave.
You pray, journal, cry, but it still lingers.

Let me show you something that flipped everything for me:

James 4:13–14 says,

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist…”

Most people think the pain is from the event.
But what if it’s not?

What if…
It’s from the future you built in your mind,
The one that never happened.
The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The version of life you were so sure God had promised.

That future felt real. But it was only an illusion.

And when it vanished, you didn’t just lose what was…
You lost what you thought would be.

That’s why the Bible urges us not to say, “I’ll do this, I’ll do that.”
But instead, “If it is the Lord’s will…”

Surrender doesn’t mean giving up.

It means breathing again.

It means waking up here instead of chasing what should’ve been.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.
And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

The #1 most common pattern I see keeping men stuck after heartbreak is breaking the first commandment… 
Let me explain.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:
“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, 
and all these things will be added to you.” 
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

why you can’t stop thinking about her.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:
“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, 
and all these things will be added to you.” 
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

Heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment 
“NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll DM you a 3-minute video to see if you qualify for one of the 10 free Clarity Calls I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover 
the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

God will keep breaking your heart until you stop believing this one lie.
Most heartbroken Christian men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because they are believing a beautiful lie.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

You might want to punch me in the face for being this brutally honest…

But if you’re hurting right now, I need you to read this all the way through.

Because most people aren’t suffering for the reason they think they are.

Not after the breakup.
Not after the job loss.
Not after the dream collapsed.

The pain hits like a truck, and you don’t know why it won’t leave.
You pray, journal, cry, but it still lingers.

Let me show you something that flipped everything for me:

James 4:13–14 says,

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist…”

Most people think the pain is from the event.
But what if it’s not?

What if…
It’s from the future you built in your mind,
The one that never happened.
The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The version of life you were so sure God had promised.

That future felt real. But it was only an illusion.

And when it vanished, you didn’t just lose what was…
You lost what you thought would be.

That’s why the Bible urges us not to say, “I’ll do this, I’ll do that.”
But instead, “If it is the Lord’s will…”

Surrender doesn’t mean giving up.

It means breathing again.

It means waking up here instead of chasing what should’ve been.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.
And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

Let me show you something that flipped everything for me:

James 4:13–14 says,

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist…”

Most people think the pain is from the event.
But what if it’s not?

What if…
It’s from the future you built in your mind,
The one that never happened.
The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The version of life you were so sure God had promised.

That future felt real. But it was only an illusion.

And when it vanished, you didn’t just lose what was…
You lost what you thought would be.

That’s why the Bible urges us not to say, “I’ll do this, I’ll do that.”
But instead, “If it is the Lord’s will…”

Surrender doesn’t mean giving up.

It means breathing again.

It means waking up here instead of chasing what should’ve been.

So next time the pain creeps in, try this prayer:
“God, not my will, but Yours.”

You’re not crazy. You’re just human.
And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern is where most 
Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment “NEXT CHAPTER” 
and I’ll DM you a 3-minute video to see if you qualify for one of the 10 free Clarity Calls 
I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover the limiting belief behind your heartbreak 
and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

top_posts.caption:
You think the problem is that you care too much.
It is not.
A lot of men who look strong in public become unstable in private for one reason: they let a woman sit in a place she was never meant to hold.
You can see it in the small things.
Checking your phone to see if she responded.
 Feeling your whole internal state change because she sounded distant.
 Reading her mood to decide whether you get to feel steady today.
 Needing her approval to feel secure again.
Most men call that love.
It is not love.
It is dependency dressed up in romantic language.
And the reason it feels so intense is because you are not just asking her for connection. You are asking her for peace. For reassurance. For identity. For emotional stability.
That is why it gets so heavy.
Because no woman was built to carry that much weight for a man.
And she can feel it.
She can feel when your peace is sitting in her hands.
 She can feel when your nervous system is asking her to stabilize you.
 She can feel when being loved by you starts to feel more like managing you.
That does not make her feel cherished.
It makes her feel responsible.
Here is the part most men never name:
You are asking her to do God’s job.
That is why the relationship starts to suffocate.
The issue is not that you love too deeply.
 The issue is that your deepest love is pointed at the wrong source.
Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.
So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife,
I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.
Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step.

God will keep breaking your heart until you stop believing this one lie.
Most men aren’t hurting because of the breakup.
They’re hurting because of the future they built in their imagination.

Let me explain…
You didn’t just lose a woman.
You lost the idol you made out of her, the life you crafted with her, 
the identity you hoped she’d validate.

That’s why it still stings even after you try to “heal”.
You can read Scripture.
You can study theology.
You can know Christian history front to back…
and still feel empty when you try to fall asleep.

Because information doesn’t heal what idolatry created.
James 4 exposes the whole thing:
“We will go here…we will do this…
We will achieve these things…”

But God says:
“You don’t even know what tomorrow will bring.”

You weren’t grieving the relationship.
You were grieving the future you assumed God would bless.
That’s why it hurts so deeply.
Not because God abandoned you.

But because He refused to let you settle for a counterfeit foundation.

Read that again.

You didn’t lose His plan.
You lost the fantasy you tried to hand to Him.
And when God tears down an idol, it feels like heartbreak.
But it’s actually mercy.

Here’s the truth most Christian men miss:
Healing isn’t passive.
Struggling is not weakness.
It’s what you need to do to step OUT of the false future you built
and INTO the sonship God is trying to form in you.

So when thoughts of her return… Pray this…

“Father… not my will. But Yours.”
That prayer will free you faster than months of overthinking, 
ghosting, hiding, or pretending you’re fine.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern 
is where most Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.

If you’re done consuming content for surface-level change, comment 
“NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll DM you a 3-minute video to see if you qualify for one of the 10 free Clarity Calls I’m doing this week, where I’ll help you uncover 
the limiting belief behind your heartbreak and map out the false hopes, habits, and blind spots that keep pulling you back into the same loop.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes. 

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

And freedom starts when you stop trying to figure it all out alone.

Understanding the problem is easy. Breaking the pattern is where most 
Christian men stay stuck for months or years.
They stay stuck because the beliefs creating the most pain are usually 
the ones they never think to question.
